revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂積濃美麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0673|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
{{Infobox Kuge | Name = Hozumi Nomimaro (穂積濃美麻呂) | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} | Birth Date = | Death Date = | Renamed = | Alias = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Grave = | Rank = {{ill|Outer Rank|ja|外位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Rank|1=gei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430321}}{{ill|Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正八位上|1=Senior Eighth Rank|lt=Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位}}? | Lord = | Clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}{{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hozumi no Oyu|ja|穂積老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Oyu|qq=}}?<ref name="unnamed-20230316131057">Genealogical records in "Hozumi Keizu" and "Mononobe no Ōmuraji Keizu," as cited in [[Ryō Ōta]]'s "Seishi Kakei Daijiten."</ref> | Siblings = | Spouse = | Children = '''{{ill|Hozumi no Shinomaro|ja|穂積忍麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Shinomaro|qq=}}''' | Notes = }}
'''Hozumi Nomimaro''' (穂積濃美麻呂) was a figure of the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, recognized as the progenitor of the powerful Hozumi clan from the Kumano region of Kii Province, which later gave rise to the {{ill|Suzuki clan|ja|鈴木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki clan|1=Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132396620}}.
== Biography == Initially residing in {{ill|Shimakami District|ja|島上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimakami District|1=Shimakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10965808}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}, in the village of Nomisato, Hozumi Nomimaro later relocated to {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|qid=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}} in {{ill|Muro District, Kii|en|Muro District, Kii|qid=Q6938811|lt=Muro District, Kii Province|1=Muro District, Kii}}. There, he served as a priest at the {{ill|Kumano Taisha|en|Kumano Taisha|qid=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Taisha|1=Kumano Taisha}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Hozumi no Oyu|ja|穂積老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Oyu|qq=}}?<ref name="unnamed-20230316131057"/> * Mother: Unknown * Spouse: Unknown * Children of unknown mother: ''' Son: {{ill|Hozumi no Shinomaro|ja|穂積忍麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Shinomaro|qq=}} - {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11594490}} of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qid=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * Junpei Suzuki, *Hozumi-sei Suzuki-shi: Kishu Tokugawa 270-nen Suzuki Kakeifu*, 1982.
== See Also == * {{ill|Fujishiro Suzuki clan|ja|藤白鈴木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Suzuki clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|穂積濃美麻呂|version=96262307|comment=Imported full ja history}}
